history.content:
<thought>
I am at row 07 column D, facing upward. The cell directly in front of me at row 06 column D is lava, so moving forward would be deadly. The cell to my right at row 07 column E is safe floor, and the open corridor extends in that direction. I should turn right to face the safe path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am still at the bottom-center, now facing right after the previous turn. The cell directly in front of me is row 07 column E, which is a wall, so I cannot move forward. The space above me at row 06 column D is safe floor, while below is outside the shown view. To face the safe corridor that goes upward, I should turn left.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing upward again at row 07 column D. The cell directly ahead at row 06 column D is lava, so moving forward is unsafe. The cell to the right at row 07 column E is safe floor and leads into the open path, so I should turn right to face it.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly ahead is a wall at row 07 column E, so moving forward is impossible. The safe open path is above me at row 06 column D. Turning left will face that safe corridor.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing upward at row 07 column D. The cell directly ahead at row 06 column D is lava, so I must not move forward. The cell to my right at row 07 column E is safe floor, so the best next step is to turn right toward that safe path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly in front of me is a wall, so I cannot move forward. The open safe route is to the left side relative to my current facing, which means I should turn left to align with the corridor at row 06 column D.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly ahead is lava, so moving forward would be deadly. The safe adjacent path is to my right, where the cell is floor. I should turn right to face that safe corridor.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly in front of me is a wall, so I cannot move forward. The safe route continues through the open space to my left relative to my current facing, so I should turn left to face that direction.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly ahead is lava, which is unsafe. The open floor cells are to my right, forming the only safe corridor away from the lava and wall. I should turn right to face that safe path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly in front is a wall, so moving forward is not possible. The safe open area lies to my left from my current facing, while the other side is blocked. I should turn left to align with the safe path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly ahead is lava, so moving forward would be fatal. The open floor cells are to my right, and the left side is blocked by a wall. Turning right is the safe move to face the open corridor.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am back to the same orientation as before: the cell directly ahead is a wall, so I cannot move forward. The safe traversable space is to my left, while the right side is blocked. Turning left is the best next step.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The view matches the earlier corridor orientation: the cell directly ahead is lava, the left side is blocked by a wall, and the open safe path is to the right. I should turn right to face the corridor.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly ahead is a wall, so moving forward is impossible. The open area is to my left, while the right side is blocked by the wall. I should turn left to face the safe path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am back to the same orientation as before. The cell directly ahead is lava, the cell to the left is a wall, and the safe route continues to the right. Turning right is the correct next step.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly in front is still a wall, so I cannot move forward. The safe unexplored route is to the left, away from the wall and not onto lava. I should turn left.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The view matches the earlier state facing upward again. The cell directly ahead is lava, and the left side is blocked by a wall. The only safe progress is to the right, so I should turn right.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing the wall directly ahead again, so moving forward is impossible. The open corridor lies to my left, while the right side is blocked by the wall. Turning left is the safe next step.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly ahead is lava, and the cell to the left is a wall. The safe route is to the right through open floor, so I should rotate clockwise to face that direction.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am back facing the wall directly in front of me, so moving forward is not possible. The open path is to my left, while the right side remains blocked by the wall. Turning left is the safest move.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly ahead is lava again, and the left side is still blocked by a wall. The only safe direction to progress is to the right through open floor, so I should turn right.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT

<thought>
The view matches the earlier position where I am facing a wall directly ahead. Forward is blocked, and the safer open route is to my left rather than into the wall side. I should turn left.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am back facing the corridor with lava directly ahead and a wall on the left. Moving forward is unsafe, so the correct move is to turn right toward the open floor cells.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly ahead is a wall, so moving forward is impossible. The open corridor lies to my left, while the right side is blocked. I should turn left to face the safe path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The view matches the earlier corridor: there is lava directly ahead, so moving forward would be deadly. The safe open path is to the right, so I should rotate clockwise to face it.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>
